title: Quiz: Introduction to Computational Drug Design
description: Test your understanding of computational drug design fundamentals

questions.question: What is the typical total time for drug discovery from target identification to approval?
questions.explanation: Drug discovery typically takes 10-15 years from initial target identification through clinical trials to market approval, with costs often exceeding $1 billion.

questions.question: Which method requires a 3D structure of the target protein?
questions.explanation: Molecular docking is a structure-based method that requires the 3D structure of the target protein to predict how ligands bind. QSAR, pharmacophore, and similarity methods are ligand-based and don't require target structure.

questions.question: When is quantum chemistry most valuable in drug design?

options.text: When electronic effects, metal coordination, or covalent binding are important
Never, force fields are always sufficient

questions.explanation: Quantum chemistry is most valuable when electronic effects matter: metal coordination in metalloproteins, covalent inhibitor design, accurate binding energies, and charge transfer interactions.

questions.question: What is a covalent inhibitor?

options.text: A drug that forms temporary bonds
A drug that forms permanent covalent bonds to the target
A drug that only uses hydrogen bonds

questions.explanation: Covalent inhibitors form permanent covalent bonds to specific residues (often cysteine) in the target protein. Examples include Ibrutinib for cancer treatment.

questions.question: Ligand-based drug design methods require knowledge of the target protein structure.
questions.explanation: False. Ligand-based methods (QSAR, pharmacophore, similarity) use information from known active compounds and don't require target structure. Structure-based methods like docking require protein structure.

questions.question: Which are non-covalent drug-target interactions? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Hydrogen bonds, salt bridges, and π-π stacking are non-covalent interactions. Acrylamide warhead binding forms a covalent bond to cysteine residues.
